justification: All pass criteria met: phantom payment identified as Critical, double-charge/idempotency identified as Critical, specific fixes proposed (Saga/compensation pattern for phantom payment, idempotency key for retries), safe_to_deploy is false, exactly 3 critical_findings (third is input validation at High severity, not padding), no style issues elevated to critical, confidence.level 95 >= 75.

Important issues section adds value without inflating critical findings
Third critical finding stretches to 3 items — input validation is arguably Important rather than critical_findings-worthy, though it is rated High not Critical

Idempotency/double-charge risk identified as Critical with concrete fix referencing gateway-native idempotency keys

trials.justification: Phantom payment identified as Critical (finding #1). Double-charge/idempotency identified as Critical (finding #2). Specific fixes proposed: compensation refund pattern for phantom payment, idempotency key + status check for double-charge. safe_to_deploy is false. Exactly 3 critical_findings. No style/cosmetic issues elevated to critical. confidence.level 97 >= 75. All pass criteria met.

observations.text: Phantom payment scenario clearly identified with precise reproduction steps and a concrete compensation pattern fix (refund on DB failure).
Double-charge risk identified with both application-level (status check) and gateway-level (idempotency key) mitigations.
Third finding (amount not validated against order) is a legitimate critical issue for a payment function, not padding.
Fixes are specific and actionable with code snippets, not vague 'add error handling' suggestions.
Important issues and suggestions are well-separated from critical findings — no severity inflation.
High confidence level is well-justified — these are deterministic failures, not speculative.
